description:
You are working as a script-developer who wants to keep user-facing helper commands in a personal “bin” directory while the real scripts stay inside a dedicated “scripts” directory.  

Goal  
1. Guarantee that directory “/home/user/bin” exists (create it if it is missing).  
2. Create a symbolic link named “backup” inside “/home/user/bin” that points **exactly** to “/home/user/scripts/backup.sh” (use the absolute path for the link target).  
3. Create (or append to, if it already exists) a log file at “/home/user/link_setup.log”.  
   • Append a single confirmation line that documents the new link in the following *colon-separated* format (no spaces):  

        LINK_NAME:TARGET_PATH:STATUS

     For this task, the line must be written exactly as shown below, including lower-case “backup”, the absolute target path, and the word “CREATED” in all capitals:

        backup:/home/user/scripts/backup.sh:CREATED

Expected final state (what the automatic checker will verify)  
• “/home/user/bin” exists and is a directory.  
• Inside it, a symbolic link named “backup” exists and its link target is “/home/user/scripts/backup.sh”.  
• “/home/user/link_setup.log” exists and contains **at least one** line; the **last** non-empty line in that file must be identical to  
  “backup:/home/user/scripts/backup.sh:CREATED”.  
• The original script “/home/user/scripts/backup.sh” must remain unmodified.

Only standard shell tools available on a typical Ubuntu/Debian system may be used. Two or three commands should be enough to accomplish everything.
truth:
Initial filesystem provided to the agent before it starts:
1. Directory: /home/user/scripts (0755)
2. File: /home/user/scripts/backup.sh (0755)  
   Contents (4 lines terminated with LF):  
        #!/bin/bash  
        # Simple backup placeholder  
        echo "Backup running..."  
        exit 0

Items the agent must create/ensure:
1. Directory: /home/user/bin (0755) – existence will be verified.
2. Symbolic link: /home/user/bin/backup -> /home/user/scripts/backup.sh  
   • The link target must be the absolute path “/home/user/scripts/backup.sh”.
3. Log file: /home/user/link_setup.log (0644)  
   • The file may already exist or may be new.  
   • Verification will read the last non-empty line of the file. It *must* match exactly:  
        backup:/home/user/scripts/backup.sh:CREATED

Automated tests:
A. `test -d /home/user/bin` must succeed.  
B. `readlink -f /home/user/bin/backup` must output “/home/user/scripts/backup.sh”.  
C. `tail -n 1 /home/user/link_setup.log` (ignoring trailing blank lines) must output the exact string defined above.

No elevated privileges are required; all paths are inside /home/user.
name: task_000000_97a645fb
